evidence_type: One of "RCT", "RWE", or "other". RCT = randomized controlled trial; RWE = real-world / observational evidence; otherwise "other". This is usually finalized later during classification - give your best read from the publication, or null if unclear.
primary_endpoint_duration_weeks: Time from intervention start to the primary endpoint assessment, in weeks (number or short string). Use null if not stated.
follow_up_after_primary_endpoint: Any additional follow-up period after the primary endpoint (e.g. "12 weeks", "6 months"), or null.
duration_additional_info: Any extra notes about study duration, timing, or assessment schedule, or null.
study_size_participants: Number of participants enrolled or analyzed (number or short string like "n=120"), or null.
intervention_and_control_group: Description of the intervention arm and the comparator/control arm (e.g. "app + usual care vs usual care"), or null.
trial_arms: Number and/or names of the trial arms (e.g. "2 arms: intervention, waitlist control"), or null.
trial_arms_additional_info: Any extra detail about the arms, randomization, or blinding, or null.
collected_data: What outcome data / measures were collected (e.g. PROMs, PHQ-9, pain level, adherence), or null.
key_outcomes_findings: The main results and conclusions of the study in one or two sentences, or null.
trial_registration_id: Trial registration identifier if any (e.g. NCT........, DRKS........, ISRCTN........), or null.
trial_start_date: Trial start date (YYYY-MM-DD or as stated), or null.
trial_end_date: Trial end / completion date (YYYY-MM-DD or as stated), or null.
publication_date: Publication date of the paper (YYYY-MM-DD or as stated), or null.
sources_publications: The real link(s) where the full research/publication was found (journal page, PubMed, DOI, ClinicalTrials.gov/DRKS, or PDF). Keep the actual URL(s), never invent them.
